sections.paragraph:
ἀποπίπτω fut. ἀποπεσοῦμαι LXX; aor. ἀπέπεσα (ἀπέπεσον LXX) (Hom.+; UPZ 70, 27 [II b.c.]; LXX). In our lit. ἀ. is used w. ἀπό and gen. (Hdt. 3, 130; Job 24:24) or w. gen. (Hdt. 3, 64; Jdth 11:6); but not simply without a gen. (as Il. 14, 351).

① lit. to fall from a point or location, fall (Jos., Ant. 6, 2) ἀ. αὐτοῦ ἀπὸ τ. ὀφθαλμῶν there fell fr. his eyes Ac 9:18. ἡ χείρ μου πυρὶ ἀποπίπτει ἀπʼ ἐμοῦ my hand, burned by fire, falls off GJs 20:1.

② fig. to depart from a norm, to deviate (Polyb.; Diod S; Jdth 11:6; Jos., Bell. 1, 527) ἀ. τῆς ὁδοῦ τ. δικαίας fall from the right way 2 Cl 5:7 (cp. Proclus, Inst. 13 ἀ. τἀγαθοῦ).—Schlageter 11. M-M.

fut. fut. = fut.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

aor. aor. = aorist

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

gen. gen. = genitive

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Il. Il. = Iliad, s. Hom.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. fr. = from

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Diod Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Schlageter Schlageter = JS., Wortschatz der ausserhalb Attikas gefundenen attischen Inschriften—List 3

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 118
